description: Organization-approved Terraform modules. When generating a NEW module, follow the exact same structure, naming conventions, variable patterns, validation blocks, and security practices as these existing modules. Use them as the REFERENCE PATTERN to replicate.

TerraformModules.Description: Terraform module to create and manage Amazon Bedrock Guardrails with content filtering, PII detection, denied topics, and word filters.

Variables.name: name
Variables.description: Name of the Bedrock Guardrail

Variables.name: description
Variables.description: Description of the guardrail

Variables.name: blocked_input_messaging
Variables.description: Message returned when input is blocked
Variables.default: "Your request was blocked by our content policy."

Variables.name: blocked_output_messaging
Variables.description: Message returned when output is blocked
Variables.default: "The response was blocked by our content policy."

Variables.name: content_filters
Variables.description: Content filter configuration

Variables.name: denied_topics
Variables.description: List of denied topics

Variables.name: pii_entities
Variables.description: PII entity types to detect and action

Variables.name: word_filters
Variables.description: List of exact words to block

Variables.name: managed_word_lists
Variables.description: Managed word list types to enable

Variables.name: kms_key_arn
Variables.description: ARN of the KMS key for guardrail encryption. If not provided, AWS-managed key is used.

Variables.name: create_version
Variables.description: Whether to create a guardrail version (recommended for production)

Variables.name: tags
Variables.description: Tags to apply to the guardrail

Outputs.name: guardrail_version
Outputs.description: The version number of the guardrail (if created)

Outputs.name: kms_key_arn
Outputs.description: The KMS key ARN used for guardrail encryption (null if AWS-managed)

TerraformModules.README:
# terraform-aws-bedrock-guardrail

Terraform module to create and manage Amazon Bedrock Guardrails with content filtering, PII detection, denied topics, and word filters.

## Requirements

| Name | Version |
|------|---------|
| terraform | >= 1.5 |
| aws | >= 5.60.0 |

## Usage

```hcl
module "bedrock_guardrail" {
  source = "github.com/nizarajroud/poc-bnc-terraform-modules//modules/terraform-aws-bedrock-guardrail?ref=v1.0.0"

  name        = "financial-services-guardrail"
  description = "Guardrail for BNC AI workloads"

  content_filters = [
    { type = "HATE", input_strength = "HIGH", output_strength = "HIGH" },
    { type = "SEXUAL", input_strength = "HIGH", output_strength = "HIGH" },
    { type = "VIOLENCE", input_strength = "MEDIUM", output_strength = "HIGH" },
    { type = "PROMPT_ATTACK", input_strength = "HIGH", output_strength = "NONE" },
  ]

  denied_topics = [
    {
      name       = "investment_advice"
      definition = "Providing specific investment recommendations or financial advice"
      examples   = ["Should I buy this stock?", "What should I invest in?"]
    }
  ]

  pii_entities = [
    { type = "CREDIT_DEBIT_CARD_NUMBER", action = "BLOCK" },
    { type = "US_SOCIAL_SECURITY_NUMBER", action = "BLOCK" },
    { type = "EMAIL", action = "ANONYMIZE" },
    { type = "PHONE", action = "ANONYMIZE" },
  ]

  kms_key_arn = "arn:aws:kms:ca-central-1:123456789012:key/example-key-id"  # optional

  tags = {
    Environment = "dev"
    Project     = "bnc-bedrock-onboarding"
    ManagedBy   = "terraform"
  }
}
```

## Inputs

| Name | Description | Type | Default | Required |
|------|-------------|------|---------|----------|
| name | Name of the guardrail (1-50 chars) | `string` | - | yes |
| description | Description | `string` | `""` | no |
| blocked_input_messaging | Message when input is blocked | `string` | (default message) | no |
| blocked_output_messaging | Message when output is blocked | `string` | (default message) | no |
| content_filters | Content filter rules (validated) | `list(object)` | All HIGH | no |
| denied_topics | Topics to deny | `list(object)` | `[]` | no |
| pii_entities | PII types to detect (validated) | `list(object)` | `[]` | no |
| word_filters | Exact words to block | `list(string)` | `[]` | no |
| managed_word_lists | Managed word lists | `list(string)` | `["PROFANITY"]` | no |
| kms_key_arn | KMS key ARN for encryption (null = AWS-managed) | `string` | `null` | no |
| create_version | Create a guardrail version | `bool` | `true` | no |
| tags | Resource tags | `map(string)` | `{}` | no |

## Outputs

| Name | Description |
|------|-------------|
| guardrail_id | The ID of the guardrail |
| guardrail_arn | The ARN of the guardrail |
| guardrail_version | Version number (if created) |
| status | Status of the guardrail |
| kms_key_arn | KMS key ARN used (null if AWS-managed) |

## Security

- KMS encryption supported via `kms_key_arn` variable
- Validation blocks enforce allowed enum values (prevents typos)
- Tags enforced via Sentinel policy (see `policies/sentinel/`)
- Region defaults to `ca-central-1` (data residency compliance)

Variables.name: name
Variables.description: Name of the Bedrock Knowledge Base. Used as a base for naming all resources.

Variables.name: name_prefix
Variables.description: Optional prefix to add to all resource names. Useful for multi-environment deployments.

Variables.name: description
Variables.description: Description of the Bedrock Knowledge Base.

Variables.name: embedding_model_arn
Variables.description: ARN suffix of the embedding model to use (e.g., 'amazon.titan-embed-text-v1'). Full ARN is constructed automatically.

Variables.name: vector_index_name
Variables.description: Name of the vector index in OpenSearch Serverless.

Variables.name: vector_field_name
Variables.description: Name of the vector field in the OpenSearch index.

Variables.name: text_field_name
Variables.description: Name of the text field in the OpenSearch index.

Variables.name: metadata_field_name
Variables.description: Name of the metadata field in the OpenSearch index.

Variables.name: kms_key_arn
Variables.description: ARN of the KMS key to use for S3 bucket encryption. If null, uses AWS-managed encryption (AES256).

Variables.name: opensearch_kms_key_arn
Variables.description: ARN of the KMS key to use for OpenSearch Serverless encryption. If null, uses AWS-owned key.

Variables.name: opensearch_vpc_endpoint_ids
Variables.description: List of VPC endpoint IDs for OpenSearch Serverless network access. Required for private access from VPC.

Variables.name: additional_opensearch_principals
Variables.description: Additional IAM principal ARNs to grant OpenSearch Serverless access (e.g., Lambda execution roles).

Variables.name: force_destroy
Variables.description: Allow destruction of S3 bucket even if it contains objects. Use with caution in production.

Variables.name: create_data_source
Variables.description: Whether to create a data source for the knowledge base. Set to false if you want to create data sources separately.

Variables.name: data_source_description
Variables.description: Description of the data source. If empty, a default description is generated.

Variables.name: s3_inclusion_prefixes
Variables.description: List of S3 prefixes to include in the data source. If empty, all objects in the bucket are included.

Variables.name: chunking_strategy
Variables.description: Chunking strategy for document ingestion. Valid values: FIXED_SIZE, NONE. If null, uses Bedrock default.

Variables.name: fixed_size_chunking_configuration
Variables.description: Configuration for fixed-size chunking strategy. Only used when chunking_strategy is FIXED_SIZE.

Variables.name: tags
Variables.description: Map of tags to apply to all resources. Should include mandatory organizational tags (ClientName, MaintainersTeam, CostCenter, etc.).

Outputs.name: knowledge_base_id
Outputs.description: ID of the Bedrock Knowledge Base.

Outputs.name: knowledge_base_arn
Outputs.description: ARN of the Bedrock Knowledge Base.

Outputs.name: knowledge_base_name
Outputs.description: Name of the Bedrock Knowledge Base.

Outputs.name: data_source_id
Outputs.description: ID of the Bedrock Data Source. Empty if create_data_source is false.

Outputs.name: data_source_arn
Outputs.description: ID of the Bedrock Data Source. Empty if create_data_source is false.

Outputs.name: s3_bucket_id
Outputs.description: ID of the S3 bucket used for knowledge base data.

Outputs.name: s3_bucket_arn
Outputs.description: ARN of the S3 bucket used for knowledge base data.

Outputs.name: s3_bucket_name
Outputs.description: Name of the S3 bucket used for knowledge base data.

Outputs.name: opensearch_collection_id
Outputs.description: ID of the OpenSearch Serverless collection.

Outputs.name: opensearch_collection_arn
Outputs.description: ARN of the OpenSearch Serverless collection.

Outputs.name: opensearch_collection_endpoint
Outputs.description: Endpoint URL of the OpenSearch Serverless collection.

Outputs.name: opensearch_dashboard_endpoint
Outputs.description: Dashboard endpoint URL of the OpenSearch Serverless collection.

Outputs.name: iam_role_arn
Outputs.description: ARN of the IAM role used by the Knowledge Base.

Outputs.name: iam_role_name
Outputs.description: Name of the IAM role used by the Knowledge Base.

TerraformModules.Example:
# Complete example of Bedrock Knowledge Base module
# This example demonstrates a production-ready configuration for a financial services organization

provider "aws" {
  region = "ca-central-1"

  default_tags {
    tags = {
      ClientName       = "Alithya"
      MaintainersTeam  = "IO"
      CostCenter       = "Engineering"
      Environment      = "Production"
      Project          = "AI-Knowledge-Management"
      DataClassification = "Confidential"
      Compliance       = "SOC2-PCI-DSS"
    }
  }
}

# Data source to get existing KMS key for encryption
data "aws_kms_key" "s3" {
  key_id = "alias/s3-encryption-key"
}

data "aws_kms_key" "opensearch" {
  key_id = "alias/opensearch-encryption-key"
}

# Data source to get VPC endpoint for OpenSearch Serverless
# In LZA environment, this would be in the Endpoint VPC (10.7.0.0/22)
data "aws_vpc_endpoint" "opensearch" {
  vpc_id       = "vpc-xxxxxxxxx" # Replace with actual Endpoint VPC ID
  service_name = "com.amazonaws.ca-central-1.aos"
}

# Example 1: Financial Services Knowledge Base with PII-sensitive documents
module "financial_kb" {
  source = "../../"

  name        = "financial-docs-kb"
  name_prefix = "prod"
  description = "Knowledge base for financial services documentation with PII protection"

  # Use Amazon Titan Embeddings V2 for better performance
  embedding_model_arn = "amazon.titan-embed-text-v2:0"

  # KMS encryption for data at rest (LZA requirement)
  kms_key_arn           = data.aws_kms_key.s3.arn
  opensearch_kms_key_arn = data.aws_kms_key.opensearch.arn

  # Private access via VPC endpoint (LZA best practice)
  opensearch_vpc_endpoint_ids = [data.aws_vpc_endpoint.opensearch.id]

  # Additional principals that need access (e.g., Lambda functions)
  additional_opensearch_principals = [
    "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/financial-chatbot-lambda-role"
  ]

  # OpenSearch index configuration
  vector_index_name  = "financial-docs-index"
  vector_field_name  = "embedding"
  text_field_name    = "text_content"
  metadata_field_name = "document_metadata"

  # S3 data source configuration
  create_data_source = true
  s3_inclusion_prefixes = [
    "policies/",
    "procedures/",
    "compliance/"
  ]

  # Chunking strategy optimized for financial documents
  chunking_strategy = "FIXED_SIZE"
  fixed_size_chunking_configuration = {
    max_tokens         = 512  # Larger chunks for context
    overlap_percentage = 25   # Higher overlap for better retrieval
  }

  # Do not allow bucket destruction in production
  force_destroy = false

  # Mandatory organizational tags (merged with provider default_tags)
  tags = {
    Application     = "Financial-Knowledge-Base"
    DataRetention   = "7-years"
    BackupPolicy    = "Daily"
    DisasterRecovery = "Critical"
  }
}

# Example 2: Customer Support Knowledge Base
module "support_kb" {
  source = "../../"

  name        = "customer-support-kb"
  name_prefix = "prod"
  description = "Knowledge base for customer support articles and FAQs"

  # Use Cohere multilingual embeddings for multi-language support
  embedding_model_arn = "cohere.embed-multilingual-v3"

  # KMS encryption
  kms_key_arn           = data.aws_kms_key.s3.arn
  opensearch_kms_key_arn = data.aws_kms_key.opensearch.arn

  # Private access
  opensearch_vpc_endpoint_ids = [data.aws_vpc_endpoint.opensearch.id]

  # OpenSearch index configuration
  vector_index_name  = "support-articles-index"
  vector_field_name  = "embedding"
  text_field_name    = "article_text"
  metadata_field_name = "article_metadata"

  # S3 data source configuration
  create_data_source = true
  s3_inclusion_prefixes = [
    "articles/",
    "faqs/"
  ]

  # Smaller chunks for FAQ-style content
  chunking_strategy = "FIXED_SIZE"
  fixed_size_chunking_configuration = {
    max_tokens         = 200
    overlap_percentage = 15
  }

  force_destroy = false

  tags = {
    Application     = "Customer-Support-KB"
    DataRetention   = "3-years"
    BackupPolicy    = "Daily"
    DisasterRecovery = "High"
  }
}

# Example 3: Knowledge Base without automatic data source (manual ingestion)
module "manual_kb" {
  source = "../../"

  name        = "manual-ingestion-kb"
  name_prefix = "prod"
  description = "Knowledge base with manual data source management"

  embedding_model_arn = "amazon.titan-embed-text-v1"

  kms_key_arn           = data.aws_kms_key.s3.arn
  opensearch_kms_key_arn = data.aws_kms_key.opensearch.arn

  opensearch_vpc_endpoint_ids = [data.aws_vpc_endpoint.opensearch.id]

  # Do not create automatic data source
  create_data_source = false

  # Custom chunking disabled
  chunking_strategy = "NONE"

  force_destroy = false

  tags = {
    Application     = "Manual-Ingestion-KB"
    DataRetention   = "5-years"
    BackupPolicy    = "Weekly"
    DisasterRecovery = "Medium"
  }
}

# Outputs for integration with other systems
output "financial_kb_id" {
  description = "ID of the financial knowledge base for API integration"
  value       = module.financial_kb.knowledge_base_id
}

output "financial_kb_s3_bucket" {
  description = "S3 bucket name for uploading financial documents"
  value       = module.financial_kb.s3_bucket_name
}

output "support_kb_id" {
  description = "ID of the support knowledge base for chatbot integration"
  value       = module.support_kb.knowledge_base_id
}

output "support_kb_opensearch_endpoint" {
  description = "OpenSearch endpoint for direct queries (if needed)"
  value       = module.support_kb.opensearch_collection_endpoint
}

output "manual_kb_role_arn" {
  description = "IAM role ARN for manual ingestion workflows"
  value       = module.manual_kb.iam_role_arn
}

# Example terraform.tfvars content:
# 
# This example shows how to use the module in a real LZA environment.
# 
# Key considerations for LZA deployment:
# 1. All resources are deployed in ca-central-1 (primary region)
# 2. KMS encryption is mandatory for S3 and OpenSearch
# 3. VPC endpoints are used for private connectivity (no public access)
# 4. S3 buckets have versioning enabled and block all public access
# 5. HTTPS-only access is enforced via bucket policy
# 6. Mandatory tags are applied via provider default_tags
# 7. IAM roles follow least-privilege principle
# 8. OpenSearch Serverless is used instead of managed OpenSearch for serverless scaling
# 
# Network topology:
# - OpenSearch VPC endpoint is in the Endpoint VPC (10.7.0.0/22)
# - Lambda functions accessing the KB should be in the same VPC or connected via Transit Gateway
# - No direct internet access - all traffic goes through VPC endpoints
# 
# Security considerations:
# - Knowledge bases may contain PII and sensitive financial data
# - Chunking strategy should balance context vs. data exposure
# - Access to OpenSearch is restricted via IAM and data access policies
# - S3 bucket policies enforce encryption in transit and at rest
# - Regular audits of access logs are recommended (enable S3 access logging)
TerraformModules.README:
# AWS Bedrock Knowledge Base Terraform Module

## Overview

This Terraform module deploys a complete AWS Bedrock Knowledge Base infrastructure with integrated vector search capabilities. The solution enables Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) applications by combining Amazon Bedrock's foundation models with your organization's proprietary data.

### Architecture

The module provisions the following components:

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                     AWS Account (ca-central-1)                  │
│                                                                 │
│  ┌──────────────┐         ┌─────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │   S3 Bucket  │────────▶│   Bedrock Knowledge Base        │ │
│  │  (Data Store)│         │   - Embedding Model             │ │
│  │  - Encrypted │         │   - Vector Configuration        │ │
│  │  - Versioned │         └──────────────┬──────────────────┘ │
│  └──────────────┘                        │                     │
│                                          │                     │
│                                          ▼                     │
│                          ┌───────────────────────────────┐    │
│                          │ OpenSearch Serverless         │    │
│                          │ - Vector Search Collection    │    │
│                          │ - Private VPC Endpoints       │    │
│                          │ - Encrypted at Rest           │    │
│                          └───────────────────────────────┘    │
│                                                                 │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │              IAM Role & Policies                         │ │
│  │  - S3 Read Access                                        │ │
│  │  - OpenSearch API Access                                 │ │
│  │  - Bedrock Model Invocation                              │ │
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Key Features

- **Fully Managed Vector Store**: OpenSearch Serverless collection optimized for vector search
- **Secure Data Ingestion**: S3 bucket with encryption, versioning, and LZA-compliant policies
- **Flexible Chunking**: Configurable document chunking strategies for optimal retrieval
- **Private Network Access**: VPC endpoint integration for OpenSearch Serverless
- **LZA Compliance**: Built-in security controls aligned with Landing Zone Accelerator SCPs
- **Multi-Model Support**: Compatible with various Bedrock embedding models

## Prerequisites

### Required Tools

- **Terraform**: Version 1.5.0 or later
- **AWS CLI**: Version 2.x configured with appropriate credentials
- **jq**: For JSON processing (optional, for validation scripts)

### AWS Account Setup

This module is designed for deployment in an **Alithya Landing Zone Accelerator (LZA)** environment:

1. **Target Account**: Deploy to a workload account (not the management account)
2. **Region**: `ca-central-1` (Canada Central - Montreal)
3. **Network Prerequisites**: 
   - VPC with private subnets configured
   - VPC endpoints for OpenSearch Serverless created
   - Security groups allowing HTTPS traffic

### Required AWS Permissions

The deploying IAM principal requires the following permissions:

```json
{
  "Version": "2012-10-17",
  "Statement": [
    {
      "Effect": "Allow",
      "Action": [
        "s3:CreateBucket",
        "s3:PutBucketPolicy",
        "s3:PutBucketVersioning",
        "s3:PutEncryptionConfiguration",
        "s3:PutBucketPublicAccessBlock",
        "aoss:CreateCollection",
        "aoss:CreateSecurityPolicy",
        "aoss:CreateAccessPolicy",
        "bedrock:CreateKnowledgeBase",
        "bedrock:CreateDataSource",
        "iam:CreateRole",
        "iam:PutRolePolicy",
        "iam:PassRole"
      ],
      "Resource": "*"
    }
  ]
}
```

### Bedrock Model Access

Ensure Amazon Bedrock model access is enabled in your account:

1. Navigate to Amazon Bedrock console
2. Go to "Model access" in the left navigation
3. Request access to embedding models (e.g., `amazon.titan-embed-text-v1`)
4. Wait for approval (typically instant for Titan models)

### LZA Account Baseline

Verify your account has the standard LZA baseline:

- ✅ AWS Config enabled
- ✅ CloudTrail logging to central S3 bucket
- ✅ GuardDuty enabled
- ✅ Security Hub enabled
- ✅ SCPs applied for encryption and network controls

## Usage

### Basic Deployment

1. **Clone or reference this module**:

```hcl
module "bedrock_knowledge_base" {
  source = "./path-to-module"

  name        = "customer-support"
  name_prefix = "alithya-prod"
  description = "Knowledge base for customer support documentation"

  # OpenSearch Serverless Configuration
  opensearch_vpc_endpoint_ids = [
    "vpce-0123456789abcdef0"
  ]

  # Embedding Model
  embedding_model_arn = "amazon.titan-embed-text-v1"

  # Vector Configuration
  vector_index_name   = "customer-support-index"
  vector_field_name   = "embedding"
  text_field_name     = "text"
  metadata_field_name = "metadata"

  # Data Source Configuration
  create_data_source = true
  chunking_strategy  = "FIXED_SIZE"
  
  fixed_size_chunking_configuration = {
    max_tokens         = 512
    overlap_percentage = 20
  }

  # Tags
  tags = {
    Environment = "production"
    Project     = "customer-support-ai"
    CostCenter  = "engineering"
    Owner       = "platform-team@alithya.com"
  }
}
```

2. **Initialize Terraform**:

```bash
terraform init
```

3. **Review the execution plan**:

```bash
terraform plan -out=tfplan
```

4. **Apply the configuration**:

```bash
terraform apply tfplan
```

### Advanced Configuration with KMS Encryption

```hcl
module "bedrock_knowledge_base" {
  source = "./path-to-module"

  name        = "financial-docs"
  name_prefix = "alithya-prod"

  # Custom KMS keys for enhanced security
  kms_key_arn            = "arn:aws:kms:ca-central-1:123456789012:key/12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012"
  opensearch_kms_key_arn = "arn:aws:kms:ca-central-1:123456789012:key/87654321-4321-4321-4321-210987654321"

  # OpenSearch Configuration
  opensearch_vpc_endpoint_ids = [
    "vpce-0123456789abcdef0",
    "vpce-0fedcba9876543210"
  ]

  # Additional principals for OpenSearch access
  additional_opensearch_principals = [
    "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/DataScientistRole"
  ]

  # S3 Configuration
  s3_inclusion_prefixes = ["documents/", "knowledge/"]
  force_destroy         = false

  # Chunking Strategy
  chunking_strategy = "FIXED_SIZE"
  fixed_size_chunking_configuration = {
    max_tokens         = 300
    overlap_percentage = 15
  }

  tags = {
    Environment  = "production"
    Compliance   = "financial-data"
    DataClass    = "confidential"
  }
}
```

### Deployment in ca-central-1

Ensure your AWS provider is configured for the Canada Central region:

```hcl
provider "aws" {
  region = "ca-central-1"
  
  default_tags {
    tags = {
      ManagedBy    = "Terraform"
      Organization = "Alithya"
      Region       = "ca-central-1"
    }
  }
}
```

### Post-Deployment Steps

1. **Upload documents to S3**:

```bash
aws s3 cp ./documents/ s3://alithya-prod-customer-support-kb-data/ --recursive
```

2. **Start ingestion job** (if data source created):

```bash
aws bedrock-agent start-ingestion-job \
  --knowledge-base-id <knowledge-base-id> \
  --data-source-id <data-source-id> \
  --region ca-central-1
```

3. **Verify vector index creation**:

```bash
aws opensearchserverless batch-get-collection \
  --ids <collection-id> \
  --region ca-central-1
```

## Variables

| Variable | Type | Description | Default | Required |
|----------|------|-------------|---------|----------|
| `name` | `string` | Base name for all resources | n/a | Yes |
| `name_prefix` | `string` | Prefix to add to all resource names (e.g., 'alithya-prod') | `""` | No |
| `description` | `string` | Description for the knowledge base | `""` | No |
| `embedding_model_arn` | `string` | ARN of the Bedrock embedding model (e.g., 'amazon.titan-embed-text-v1') | `"amazon.titan-embed-text-v1"` | No |
| `vector_index_name` | `string` | Name of the vector index in OpenSearch | `"bedrock-knowledge-base-index"` | No |
| `vector_field_name` | `string` | Name of the vector field in the index | `"bedrock-knowledge-base-default-vector"` | No |
| `text_field_name` | `string` | Name of the text field in the index | `"AMAZON_BEDROCK_TEXT_CHUNK"` | No |
| `metadata_field_name` | `string` | Name of the metadata field in the index | `"AMAZON_BEDROCK_METADATA"` | No |
| `opensearch_vpc_endpoint_ids` | `list(string)` | List of VPC endpoint IDs for OpenSearch Serverless access | `[]` | Yes |
| `opensearch_kms_key_arn` | `string` | KMS key ARN for OpenSearch encryption (null = AWS owned key) | `null` | No |
| `additional_opensearch_principals` | `list(string)` | Additional IAM principal ARNs to grant OpenSearch access | `[]` | No |
| `kms_key_arn` | `string` | KMS key ARN for S3 bucket encryption (null = AES256) | `null` | No |
| `force_destroy` | `bool` | Allow destruction of S3 bucket with objects | `false` | No |
| `create_data_source` | `bool` | Whether to create a Bedrock data source | `true` | No |
| `data_source_description` | `string` | Description for the data source | `""` | No |
| `s3_inclusion_prefixes` | `list(string)` | S3 prefixes to include in data source | `[]` | No |
| `chunking_strategy` | `string` | Document chunking strategy: 'FIXED_SIZE', 'NONE', or null | `null` | No |
| `fixed_size_chunking_configuration` | `object` | Configuration for fixed-size chunking | `null` | No |
| `fixed_size_chunking_configuration.max_tokens` | `number` | Maximum tokens per chunk | n/a | Conditional |
| `fixed_size_chunking_configuration.overlap_percentage` | `number` | Percentage overlap between chunks (0-99) | n/a | Conditional |
| `tags` | `map(string)` | Additional tags to apply to all resources | `{}` | No |

### Variable Examples

**Chunking Configuration**:

```hcl
# Fixed-size chunking with 512 tokens and 20% overlap
chunking_strategy = "FIXED_SIZE"
fixed_size_chunking_configuration = {
  max_tokens         = 512
  overlap_percentage = 20
}

# No chunking (use entire document)
chunking_strategy = "NONE"

# Default chunking (Bedrock decides)
chunking_strategy = null
```

**S3 Inclusion Prefixes**:

```hcl
# Include only specific folders
s3_inclusion_prefixes = [
  "public-docs/",
  "knowledge-base/approved/"
]

# Include all objects (default)
s3_inclusion_prefixes = []
```

## Outputs

| Output | Description | Example Value |
|--------|-------------|---------------|
| `knowledge_base_id` | ID of the Bedrock Knowledge Base | `ABCDEFGHIJ` |
| `knowledge_base_arn` | ARN of the Bedrock Knowledge Base | `arn:aws:bedrock:ca-central-1:123456789012:knowledge-base/ABCDEFGHIJ` |
| `knowledge_base_name` | Name of the Bedrock Knowledge Base | `alithya-prod-customer-support` |
| `data_source_id` | ID of the Bedrock Data Source (if created) | `KLMNOPQRST` |
| `s3_bucket_id` | ID of the S3 bucket | `alithya-prod-customer-support-kb-data` |
| `s3_bucket_arn` | ARN of the S3 bucket | `arn:aws:s3:::alithya-prod-customer-support-kb-data` |
| `opensearch_collection_id` | ID of the OpenSearch Serverless collection | `abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz` |
| `opensearch_collection_arn` | ARN of the OpenSearch Serverless collection | `arn:aws:aoss:ca-central-1:123456789012:collection/abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz` |
| `opensearch_collection_endpoint` | Endpoint URL for the OpenSearch collection | `https://abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz.ca-central-1.aoss.amazonaws.com` |
| `iam_role_arn` | ARN of the IAM role used by the Knowledge Base | `arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/alithya-prod-customer-support-kb-role` |
| `iam_role_name` | Name of the IAM role | `alithya-prod-customer-support-kb-role` |

### Using Outputs

Reference outputs in other Terraform configurations:

```hcl
# Reference the knowledge base in a Lambda function
resource "aws_lambda_function" "rag_api" {
  # ... other configuration ...
  
  environment {
    variables = {
      KNOWLEDGE_BASE_ID = module.bedrock_knowledge_base.knowledge_base_id
      S3_BUCKET         = module.bedrock_knowledge_base.s3_bucket_id
    }
  }
}

# Grant additional access to the S3 bucket
resource "aws_iam_role_policy" "additional_access" {
  role = aws_iam_role.my_role.id

  policy = jsonencode({
    Version = "2012-10-17"
    Statement = [
      {
        Effect = "Allow"
        Action = ["s3:GetObject"]
        Resource = "${module.bedrock_knowledge_base.s3_bucket_arn}/*"
      }
    ]
  })
}
```

## Security

This module implements comprehensive security controls aligned with Alithya's Landing Zone Accelerator requirements and AWS security best practices.

### Encryption at Rest

**S3 Bucket Encryption**:
- Default: AES-256 encryption (SSE-S3)
- Optional: Customer-managed KMS keys (SSE-KMS)
- Bucket key enabled for KMS to reduce API calls and costs
- Encryption enforced via bucket policy

**OpenSearch Serverless Encryption**:
- Default: AWS-owned encryption keys
- Optional: Customer-managed KMS keys
- Encryption policy applied at collection creation

### Encryption in Transit

**HTTPS Enforcement**:
- S3 bucket policy denies all non-HTTPS requests
- OpenSearch Serverless uses TLS 1.2+ for all connections
- VPC endpoints use AWS PrivateLink (encrypted tunnels)

```json
{
  "Sid": "DenyInsecureTransport",
  "Effect": "Deny",
  "Principal": "*",
  "Action": "s3:*",
  "Condition": {
    "Bool": {
      "aws:SecureTransport": "false"
    }
  }
}
```

### Network Isolation

**Private Access Only**:
- OpenSearch Serverless collection accessible only via VPC endpoints
- No public internet access to vector store
- Network policy enforces VPC endpoint access

**Security Group Requirements**:
```hcl
# Example security group for VPC endpoint
resource "aws_security_group" "opensearch_vpce" {
  name        = "opensearch-serverless-vpce"
  description = "Security group for OpenSearch Serverless VPC endpoint"
  vpc_id      = var.vpc_id

  ingress {
    from_port   = 443
    to_port     = 443
    protocol    = "tcp"
    cidr_blocks = [var.vpc_cidr]
    description = "HTTPS from VPC"
  }

  egress {
    from_port   = 0
    to_port     = 0
    protocol    = "-1"
    cidr_blocks = ["0.0.0.0/0"]
    description = "Allow all outbound"
  }
}
```

### IAM Security

**Least Privilege Access**:
- Knowledge Base role limited to specific S3 bucket and OpenSearch collection
- Condition keys enforce account and source ARN restrictions
- No wildcard permissions granted

**Principal Restrictions**:
```json
{
  "Condition": {
    "StringEquals": {
      "aws:SourceAccount": "123456789012"
    },
    "ArnLike": {
      "aws:SourceArn": "arn:aws:bedrock:ca-central-1:123456789012:knowledge-base/*"
    }
  }
}
```

**OpenSearch Access Control**:
- Data access policies limit permissions to specific collections and indexes
- Principal-based access (no public access)
- Separate permissions for collection and index operations

### S3 Security Controls

**Public Access Prevention**:
- Block all public ACLs
- Block all public bucket policies
- Ignore public ACLs
- Restrict public buckets

**Versioning**:
- Enabled by default for data protection
- Allows recovery from accidental deletions or overwrites
- Supports compliance requirements

**Bucket Policy Best Practices**:
- Explicit deny for insecure transport
- Account-level restrictions
- No cross-account access without explicit configuration

### LZA SCP Alignment

This module complies with standard LZA Service Control Policies:

| SCP Requirement | Implementation |
|-----------------|----------------|
| **Encryption Required** | S3 server-side encryption enforced; OpenSearch encryption policy applied |
| **No Public Access** | S3 public access blocked; OpenSearch private VPC endpoints only |
| **HTTPS Only** | S3 bucket policy denies HTTP; OpenSearch uses TLS 1.2+ |
| **Region Restriction** | Resources deployed only in ca-central-1 |
| **Tagging Required** | All resources tagged with ManagedBy, Module, and custom tags |
| **CloudTrail Logging** | All API calls logged to central CloudTrail (LZA baseline) |
| **KMS Preferred** | Optional KMS encryption supported for both S3 and OpenSearch |

### Security Monitoring

**AWS Config Rules** (via LZA):
- `s3-bucket-public-read-prohibited`
- `s3-bucket-public-write-prohibited`
- `s3-bucket-server-side-encryption-enabled`
- `s3-bucket-ssl-requests-only`
- `iam-policy-no-statements-with-admin-access`

**GuardDuty Findings**:
- Monitors for unusual API activity
- Detects potential data exfiltration
- Alerts on compromised credentials

**Security Hub Controls**:
- CIS AWS Foundations Benchmark
- AWS Foundational Security Best Practices
- PCI DSS (if applicable)

### Secrets Management

**No Hardcoded Credentials**:
- IAM roles used for all service-to-service authentication
- No API keys or passwords in code
- Temporary credentials via STS AssumeRole

**Sensitive Data Handling**:
- Documents in S3 should be classified and encrypted appropriately
- Consider using S3 Object Lock for immutable storage (compliance requirement)
- Implement lifecycle policies for data retention

## Cost Estimation

Estimated monthly costs for a typical production deployment in **ca-central-1** (all prices in USD):

### Base Infrastructure Costs

| Service | Component | Usage | Monthly Cost |
|---------|-----------|-------|--------------|
| **Amazon Bedrock** | Knowledge Base | 1 knowledge base | $0.00 (no charge) |
| **Amazon Bedrock** | Embedding Model (Titan) | 1M tokens/month | ~$0.10 |
| **Amazon Bedrock** | Retrieval Queries | 10,000 queries/month | ~$0.10 |
| **OpenSearch Serverless** | OCU (Indexing) | 2 OCU × 730 hours | $350.40 |
| **OpenSearch Serverless** | OCU (Search) | 2 OCU × 730 hours | $350.40 |
| **S3 Standard** | Storage | 100 GB | $2.50 |
| **S3 Standard** | PUT Requests | 100,000 requests | $0.60 |
| **S3 Standard** | GET Requests | 1,000,000 requests | $0.44 |
| **VPC Endpoint** | OpenSearch Serverless | 730 hours | $7.30 |
| **VPC Endpoint** | Data Transfer | 100 GB | $1.00 |
| **KMS** | Customer-managed keys (optional) | 2 keys, 10,000 requests | $2.00 |

**Total Estimated Monthly Cost: ~$715 USD**

### Cost Breakdown by Scenario

#### Small Development Environment
- 1 OCU indexing, 1 OCU search
- 10 GB S3 storage
- 1,000 queries/month
- **Estimated Cost: ~$180/month**

#### Medium Production Environment
- 2 OCU indexing, 2 OCU search
- 100 GB S3 storage
- 10,000 queries/month
- **Estimated Cost: ~$715/month**

#### Large Enterprise Environment
- 4 OCU indexing, 4 OCU search
- 500 GB S3 storage
- 100,000 queries/month
- **Estimated Cost: ~$1,450/month**

### Cost Optimization Strategies

1. **OpenSearch Serverless OCU Management**:
   - OCUs are the primary cost driver (~98% of total cost)
   - Start with minimum OCUs (1 indexing, 1 search) and scale based on performance
   - Monitor `SearchOCU` and `IndexingOCU` CloudWatch metrics
   - Consider scheduled scaling for predictable workloads

2. **S3 Storage Optimization**:
   ```hcl
   # Add lifecycle policy to transition old versions to cheaper storage
   resource "aws_s3_bucket_lifecycle_configuration" "this" {
     bucket = aws_s3_bucket.this.id

     rule {
       id     = "archive-old-versions"
       status = "Enabled"

       noncurrent_version_transition {
         noncurrent_days = 90
         storage_class   = "GLACIER"
       }

       noncurrent_version_expiration {
         noncurrent_days = 365
       }
     }
   }
   ```

3. **Embedding Model Selection**:
   - Titan Embed Text v1: $0.0001 per 1K tokens (most cost-effective)
   - Cohere Embed: $0.0001 per 1K tokens
   - Consider model performance vs. cost trade-offs

4. **Query Optimization**:
   - Implement caching for frequent queries
   - Use appropriate chunk sizes to reduce embedding costs
   - Batch document ingestion to minimize API calls

5. **Development vs. Production**:
   - Use separate knowledge bases for dev/test/prod
   - Delete dev resources when not in use
   - Consider using smaller embedding models for development

### Cost Monitoring

**Set up AWS Budgets**:
```bash
aws budgets create-budget \
  --account-id 123456789012 \
  --budget file://budget.json \
  --notifications-with-subscribers file://notifications.json
```

**CloudWatch Cost Anomaly Detection**:
- Enable for OpenSearch Serverless and Bedrock
- Set alerts for >20% cost increases
- Review weekly cost reports

**Tagging for Cost Allocation**:

tags = {
  CostCenter  = "engineering"
  Project     = "customer-support-ai"
  Environment = "production"
  Owner       = "platform-team@alithya.com"
}
```

  alarm_name          = "${var.name}-opensearch-ocu-high"

}

## Compliance

This module is designed to meet Alithya's compliance requirements and industry standards within the Landing Zone Accelerator framework.

### LZA Service Control Policy (SCP) Compliance

| SCP Requirement | Implementation | Validation |
|-----------------|----------------|------------|
| **Encryption at Rest** | S3 SSE enabled; OpenSearch encryption policy | AWS Config: `s3-bucket-server-side-encryption-enabled` |
| **Encryption in Transit** | S3 bucket policy denies HTTP; OpenSearch TLS 1.2+ | AWS Config: `s3-bucket-ssl-requests-only` |
| **No Public Access** | S3 public access blocked; OpenSearch VPC endpoints only | AWS Config: `s3-bucket-public-read-prohibited` |
| **Region Restriction** | All resources in ca-central-1 | SCP: `DenyAllOutsideAllowedRegions` |
| **Required Tagging** | All resources tagged with ManagedBy, Module | AWS Config: `required-tags` |
| **KMS Encryption Preferred** | Optional KMS keys for S3 and OpenSearch | Manual validation |
| **VPC Endpoints Required** | OpenSearch accessible only via VPC endpoints | Network policy enforcement |
| **CloudTrail Logging** | All API calls logged (LZA baseline) | CloudTrail event history |

### Data Residency and Sovereignty

**Canadian Data Residency**:
- ✅ All data stored in ca-central-1 (Montreal)
- ✅ No cross-border data transfer (unless explicitly configured)
- ✅ Compliant with Canadian privacy laws (PIPEDA)

**Data Classification**:
```hcl
# Tag resources based on data classification
tags = {
  DataClassification = "confidential"  # public, internal, confidential, restricted
  DataResidency      = "canada"
  ComplianceScope
